title: 8 Herbal Creams For Eye Swelling
intro_desc: Herbal creams can help ease eye swelling, and they work in a couple of ways. When you apply a cream to the affected area, the active ingredients get absorbed into the skin and start to calm down the inflammation. This is why herbal teas can also relieve eye swelling - the same active ingredients are present in the leaves, flowers, or roots of the plants.  For instance, Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling. When you apply a calendula cream to your eye, it can help ease the puffiness and discomfort. Another herb, Hamamelis virginiana or witch hazel, has a compound called tannin that helps constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling. By applying a witch hazel cream to your eye, you can help bring down the swelling and ease the discomfort.  Symphytum officinale, or comfrey, is another herb that can help with eye swelling. Comfrey cream has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and promote healing. When you apply a comfrey cream to your eye, it can help ease the puffiness and discomfort, and even promote the healing of any underlying injuries or conditions that may be causing the swelling.  Using herbal creams for eye swelling can be beneficial because they're often gentle and non-irritating, even for sensitive skin. They can also be used in combination with other treatments, like cold compresses or eye drops, to help alleviate the symptoms of eye swelling. Plus, many herbal creams are made with natural ingredients, which can be a relief for people who are looking for chemical-free solutions to their eye problems.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and redness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and edema by blocking the action of pro-inflammatory molecules. The carotenoids, including lutein and zeaxanthin, have antioxidant properties that help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the skin, Calendula officinalis creams can help alleviate eye swelling and promote a more comfortable and healthy appearance.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the Calendula flowers in the coconut oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Melt the shea butter and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until smooth.
Strain the infused coconut oil and discard the Calendula flowers. Add the vitamin E oil and stir well. Combine the infused coconut oil mixture with the melted shea butter and beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use as needed to reduce eye swelling and soothe the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams contains tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and protect the delicate skin around the eyes. The tannins in Hamamelis virginiana creams constrict blood vessels, reducing the flow of blood to the affected area and minimizing swelling. The flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and irritation. By applying Hamamelis virginiana creams to the affected area, you can help alleviate eye swelling and promote a speedy recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Hamamelis virginiana herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hamamelis virginiana leaves and 1 cup of cold carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass jar.
Steep the dried leaves in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the herbs infuse into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax and shea butter melt and the mixture is smooth. Let cool and use as needed for eye swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains a high concentration of allantoin, aloe-emodin, and rosmarinic acid. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation. Allantoin is particularly effective in soothing and calming irritated tissues, while aloe-emodin has antioxidant properties that help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes. Rosmarinic acid also contributes to the anti-inflammatory effects of Symphytum officinale creams, making it a valuable ingredient for treating eye swelling. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Symphytum officinale creams provide relief from eye swelling and promote a return to normal appearance.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 tablespoons of dried Symphytum officinale leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Symphytum officinale leaves to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the leaves.
Mix in the melted beeswax and let the mixture cool and solidify before applying it to the affected area to reduce eye swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents like glycoproteins, polysaccharides, and vitamins that help reduce eye swelling. Glycoproteins in aloe vera have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. Polysaccharides in aloe vera, particularly acemannan, have been shown to inhibit inflammatory mediators, further reducing eye swelling. The vitamins, including vitamin A, B, and E, in aloe vera have antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes. Aloe vera's soothing and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce eye swelling and provide relief from puffiness and redness.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, and 1 teaspoon of tea tree oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds.
Add the Aloe barbadensis gel, vitamin E oil, and tea tree oil to the melted mixture. Stir well until combined.
Remove from heat and let cool until the mixture thickens and reaches a creamy consistency.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let cool completely before use. Store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules. The alkylamides, specifically echinacoside and isobutylamides, also have a soothing effect on the skin, reducing redness and puffiness. Caffeic acid has antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and swelling. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide relief from eye swelling and promote a more comfortable and healthy appearance.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Steep the dried flowers in the carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the infused oil. Heat the mixture in the double boiler or over low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool. Your Echinacea purpurea cream is now ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area for eye swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains a high concentration of silymarin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. Silymarin works by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can help to alleviate eye swelling. The flavonoids present in Silybum marianum, such as kaempferol and isorhapontigenin, also have anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, silymarin has antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce cell damage, which can contribute to eye swelling. By applying Silybum marianum creams to the affected area, these bioactive compounds can be absorbed into the skin and help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until melted.
Add the dried Silybum marianum flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Add the beeswax and vitamin E oil, and heat until the beeswax melts.

Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for applying to the skin. Pour the mixture into a small tin or container and store it in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hyperforin and hypericin, two active constituents that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which cause pain and swelling. Hyperforin also has antihistamine effects, which can alleviate itchy and watery eyes often associated with swelling. The flavonoids present in Hypericum perforatum creams, such as rutin and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage. By applying Hypericum perforatum creams to the affected area, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help alleviate eye swelling and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin E oil in a small saucepan. Heat gently until the beeswax melts, then pour into a clean container and let it cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains gingerol and shogaol, two active constituents known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and LOX. Gingerol and shogaol also have potent antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate eye swelling caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or eczema. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of gingerol and shogaol in Zingiber officinale creams make it a potential remedy for eye swelling.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for eye swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycerin in a saucepan.
Add 1 tablespoon of grated ginger (Zingiber officinale) to the saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Mix in 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil. Store the cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 week.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce eye swelling, but it depends on the type of tea. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that might ease puffiness. These teas work by calming the body and reducing fluid buildup, which can contribute to swelling.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to ease eye swelling. Some teas, like chamomile and peppermint, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce puffiness. However, consuming them daily can cause interactions with medications or worsen certain health conditions. It's best to drink them in moderation and observe how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like chamomile and calendula may help reduce eye swelling. They typically start working within 15 to 30 minutes after consumption. The swelling may start to go down, and the area may feel cooler and less inflamed. The effects can last for a few hours, depending on the individual and the severity of the swelling.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for eye swelling can be most effective in the morning. This is because you'll likely be at your most puffy, and the tea can help reduce the swelling. Plus, starting your day with a soothing brew can be a great way to calm your eyes and get your day off to a relaxed start.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for eye swelling. Some teas, like ginkgo biloba and saw palmetto, may increase the risk of bleeding, which can worsen eye swelling. Others, like feverfew and ginger, may affect blood pressure or thin the blood, also potentially worsening eye swelling. Be cautious with herbal tea use if you take prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and your eyes are puffy, you might think herbal teas can help. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can be safe in small amounts. But others, like those with foxglove or pennyroyal, can be bad for you and your baby. So, check what's in the tea before you drink it.
faq_breastfeeding: When you're breastfeeding and experiencing eye swelling, herbal teas might be tempting to try. However, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like those containing chamomile or peppermint, can affect milk supply or interact with other substances. It's also possible to be allergic to certain herbs. If you do choose to try herbal teas, start with small amounts and monitor your body's reaction.
title_2: The Ultimate Guide: 8 Herbal Creams for Athletes to Manage Eye Swelling
